blocks.text: watched Julian walk away. She had made a decision. She just hadn't finished making it.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: I've spoken with all five. They're staying for now.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Any of them give you the argument?

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Everyone heard it. Nobody claims to know who.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Cade said it wasn't him. The others didn't volunteer anything.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Nadia Reyes says she was downstairs the entire evening. Wren Cole confirms it.
Confirmed by the only other person in the group who notices things. Either Wren was telling the truth or she had a reason to say it.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Dean Ashby and Sophie Laurent were together. They confirm each other.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Cade says he was outside alone for thirty minutes. Around ten. No one confirms it.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: He had a conversation with Marcus earlier. He called it old stuff.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: It was placed. Not set down. The exact center of the nightstand. Someone put it there deliberately.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: The rest of the room wasn't neat. Clothes on the chair. Shoes left at the door. Belt still in the loops on the pants he'd worn. But the glass was centered exactly.

blocks.prompt: The water glass. Who placed it there?

options.label: Marcus put it there himself. It means nothing.
Someone placed it before he got there. Before he even went upstairs.
